sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
OK so clicking on the white dot only changed the progress bar at the bottom (W changed from 39 to 38 pixels, G changed from 601 to 602 pixels). The left-side objects didn't change at all. So the white dots are not interactive for gameplay purposes - they're just part of the divider.
Let me think about this more carefully. I've been trying to understand the game mechanics, but I'm not making progress. Let me try a completely different approach.
Looking at the current state:
- Red object: vertical at [24,18]-[29,21]
- Blue square: top at [20,18]-[23,21]
- Green square: middle-center at [40,10]-[41,11]
The initial state was:
- Red object: horizontal at [30,12]-[33,17]
- Blue square: top at [20,18]-[23,21]
- Green square: middle-center at [40,10]-[41,11]
So the only difference is the red object position. The red target toggles the red object between horizontal and vertical.
Let me think about what the goal might be. Maybe the goal is to have:
1. Red object in a specific position (horizontal or vertical)
2. Blue square in a specific position (top or bottom)
3. Green square in a specific position (one of 9 positions)
I've tried several combinations, but none of them completed the level. Let me try a systematic approach.
Actually, wait. Let me re-examine the game. I notice that the left side has:
- A gray box with a yellow square (top)
- A blue square (top position)
- A red object (vertical or horizontal)
- A dark red square (bottom position)
- A gray square (bottom-left)
- A gray box with a green square (bottom)
And the right side has:
- A red cross shape (top)
- A blue cross shape (bottom)
Maybe the goal is to have the left side objects match the right side cross shapes in some way. For example:
- The red object should match the red cross shape (vertical)
- The blue square should match the blue cross shape (bottom)
- The green square should be in a specific position
But I've already tried red vertical + blue top + green middle-center, and it didn't work. Let me try red vertical + blue bottom + green middle-center.
Actually, wait. Let me re-examine the blue target behavior. When I click on the blue target, does it swap the blue square with the checkerboard pattern? Or does it move the blue square to a different position?
Let me check the history to see what happened when I clicked on the blue target.
Actually, I don't have access to the full history. Let me just try clicking on the blue target and see what happens.
Current state:
- Red object: vertical
- Blue square: top
- Green square: middle-center
Let me try clicking on the blue target to swap the blue square to the bottom position. Then I'll try all 9 green positions with red vertical and blue bottom.
Actually, let me first try to understand the blue target behavior better. Let me click on the blue target and see what happens.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: White dots are just dividers, not interactive. Only right-side targets (red/blue crosses) and left-side gray box are interactive. Red target toggles red object shape, blue target swaps blue/checkerboard positions, gray box moves green square. Progress bar at bottom shrinks with each action.
Goal model: Need to find winning configuration among 36 states (2 red × 2 blue × 9 green). Current: red vertical, blue top, green middle-center.
Plan: Try blue bottom + red vertical + all 9 green positions systematically. First click blue target to swap blue square.
